touches.ai_summary: Initial LinkedIn outreach to Isaac Wagner. Rep introduced AskElephant's onboarding automation capabilities for fintech teams. Isaac responded positively, expressing interest in learning more about how it could reduce manual CS work.

questions_asked.question: Does this integrate with our existing ticketing system?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have native integrations with all major platforms and can custom-build connectors if needed.

next_steps: Schedule intro call to discuss current onboarding pain points

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent with case study showing 40% reduction in onboarding time for similar-sized fintech company. Isaac confirmed availability for discovery call next week.

questions_asked.question: What's the typical implementation timeline?
questions_asked.answer: Most SMB customers are fully onboarded within 2-3 weeks with our guided setup process.

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call with Isaac covering current onboarding workflow. SolarSpark manually processes 15-20 new clients monthly, taking 3-4 hours per client. Isaac sees clear value but mentioned budget constraints and wanting to involve his team before moving forward.

questions_asked.question: Walk me through your current onboarding process from contract signature to first transaction.
questions_asked.answer: We have a 12-step checklist that CS manually works through—KYC docs, account setup, API keys, training calls. Very manual, lots of back-and-forth emails.

questions_asked.question: What would success look like for you in 6 months?
questions_asked.answer: Cut onboarding time in half, free up CS team to focus on expansion accounts instead of repetitive setup tasks.

questions_asked.question: Can we start with a smaller pilot before committing to all 6 seats?
questions_asked.answer: We can structure the engagement flexibly, though pricing improves with the 6-seat tier.

objections: Budget is tight this quarter, need to make sure ROI is clear
next_steps: Isaac to loop in Michael from RevOps for technical review

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up call with Isaac to review ROI projections. Discussed bringing in Michael Martin (RevOps) and Hazel Hall (Account Manager) for technical validation. Isaac confirmed budget exists but mentioned needing CFO approval for anything over $3k.

questions_asked.question: The ROI model shows 200 hours saved annually—is that realistic for our volume?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, that's conservative based on your 15-20 clients per month. Some customers see even higher gains once they optimize workflows.

questions_asked.question: Who typically signs off on CS tooling purchases at SolarSpark?
questions_asked.answer: For this budget range, I need CFO approval. Our VP of CS left last month so I'm acting in that capacity temporarily.

next_steps: Schedule demo with Michael and Hazel
Isaac to check with finance on Q2 budget

touches.ai_summary: Email sent with demo recording and technical documentation for Michael and Hazel to review. Isaac confirmed they're evaluating but mentioned timeline pressure to decide by end of month due to Q2 planning cycles.
next_steps: Team to review materials by Friday
Schedule decision call for next week

touches.ai_summary: Demo call with Isaac, Michael, and Hazel. Michael validated technical fit and integration requirements. Hazel asked detailed workflow questions. Isaac confirmed this solves their problem but expressed concern about list price. No executive sponsor on the call despite being marked as exec meeting.

questions_asked.question: How does the automation handle edge cases where clients need custom onboarding steps?
questions_asked.answer: The workflow engine supports conditional logic and custom branching—you can build exception paths for VIP clients or unique requirements.

questions_asked.question: What level of support is included, and can we get dedicated onboarding help?
questions_asked.answer: Unlimited tier includes priority support and dedicated onboarding. We'll assign a CSM to guide your setup.

questions_asked.question: Are there other tools you're comparing this against?
questions_asked.answer: Not actively—we looked at building in-house but don't have the eng resources. This is the only external option we're seriously considering.

objections: Pricing is higher than we budgeted—is there flexibility on the $6k list price?
next_steps: Rep to discuss pricing options with leadership
Isaac to get final CFO approval once pricing is confirmed

touches.ai_summary: Pricing negotiation call with Isaac. Discussed budget constraints and Q2 timing. Rep offered 41% discount to hit $3,500 to close before quarter-end. Isaac agreed to push for CFO signature this week with discounted rate and 6-seat commitment.

questions_asked.question: If we sign by Friday, can you hold this price for a Q3 expansion if we need more seats?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can lock this per-seat rate for any additions within the next 6 months.

questions_asked.question: What's your confidence level on getting CFO sign-off at $3,500?
questions_asked.answer: High—this fits within our CS tools budget if we can close it before month-end. I'll push it through.

next_steps: Rep to send revised proposal at $3,500
Isaac to route to CFO for signature by Friday
